channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1077)—Answers for Saturday, May 23
item.content:encoded:
Welcome to another NYT Connections guide! In this handy walkthrough, you’ll find the best NYT Connections hints, answers, and tips anywhere online. In just a matter of seconds, you can smash today’s NYT Connections puzzle with ease, putting an end to any struggles you might be having with it!

Scroll down to get the solutions you need right now!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (May 23)

Here are the words for today’s NYT Connections:

  • FIRST
  • EMPIRE
  • LAST
  • BEEHIVE
  • HAWKEYE
  • SOONER
  • FORCE
  • WOLVERINE
  • PHANTOM
  • POMPADOUR
  • DAREDEVIL
  • RATHER
  • CHIGNON
  • NIGHTCRAWLER
  • PREFERABLY
  • BOUFFANT

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Big, bold hairstyles
  • Green – More readily available
  • Blue – Marvel characters
  • Purple – Star Wars title terms

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, May 23!

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, May 23 (#1077)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – BEEHIVE, BOUFFANT, CHIGNON, POMPADOUR
  • Green – FIRST, PREFERABLY, RATHER, SOONER
  • Blue – DAREDEVIL, HAWKEYE, NIGHTCRAWLER, WOLVERINE
  • Purple – EMPIRE, FORCE, LAST, PHANTOM

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: Dead by Daylight Reveal Teaser Has Thousands of People Watching a Cabin in the Woods
item.content:encoded:
Behavior Interactive is lining up some kind of Dead by Daylight reveal, and for the last six hours, it has led to tens of thousands of people watching a barely moving teaser image of a cabin in the woods. On Twitch, the Dead by Daylight channel went live, hosting what many thought was a still image of a small windowed cabin in a dark forest, but it quickly became obvious it was a slow-burning video.

In the hours that passed, users picked out almost imperceptible shapes and figures in the distance, called out movement in the trees, and referred to video glitches as surefire signs of what’s coming next to Dead by Daylight.

What’s Coming to Dead by Daylight?

Many have theorized that Behavior Interactive is teasing the imminent arrival of a new killer, and thanks to the setting, they believe it’s something related to Slenderman, or some similar entity.

Here’s the stream, but whether it’s still running or not by the time you see this is another thing entirely:

Dead by Daylight’s tenth anniversary is on the horizon, so it stands to reason that something big will be landing in the survival horror title. Over the last decade, DBD has become a staple of the horror space, pulling in millions of fans and securing a diehard community that happily eats up every new update Behavior Interactive drops.

At one point, a peak of around 37,000 concurrent viewers were watching this ghostly visage unfold, wondering what on Earth the team could be about to reveal.

I’ll tell you this, it’s tough knowing what’s coming and not saying a word!

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server what you think is coming next to Dead by Daylight, and stay tuned.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news about Sony’s next State of Play presentation

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Square Enix Needs to Modernize Its IP, Former Exec Says
item.content:encoded:
Jacob Navok, former Director of Business at Square Enix, is discussing his belief that the company has failed to bring its popular IPs, such as Final Fantasy, to new generations by truly embracing the live-service model. In a long Twitter post, Navok admits that the brand actually understands live service as they’ve proven that with games like Final Fantasy 14 and Final Fantasy 11.

Still, he believes the popular JRPG franchise could do a better job of meeting young people where they’re at, for example, he hypothesizes the fun that a K-pop Demon Hunters x Final Fantasy collaboration could be.

Square Enix Accused of Failing to Reach New Generation of Gamers By Former Exec

As further examples of what he thinks Square Enix should do with the Final Fantasy brand, Navok discusses the success that Magic: The Gathering has had outside of its original medium. He also uses Kingdom Hearts as an example, saying that the series has shown how Final Fantasy lore could potentially mesh with other properties. He also says he thinks it would be interesting to see Final Fantasy 14 used as the technical basis for building a new free-to-play experience that continuously updates through modern collaborations.

“I think Square Enix is so used to being slow that it can’t imagine what life would be like if it acted fast. There’s a lot of internal red tape; the CEO’s job is to make a decision and cut that red tape so that innovation flourishes,” he said.

While it’s no Fortnite, Final Fantasy 11 continues to go strong nearly 25 years later. Recently, the game director discussed the possibility of adding a new area to the game. Read his comments here. As for Navok, he currently works at the AI Production Studio called Genvid.

What do you think of these remarks? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: McDonald’s Japan Has a New Mega Man Crossover Campaign
item.content:encoded:
McDonald’s in Japan has launched a new crossover campaign with Mega Man. The promotion is intended to spotlight the premium roast iced coffee.

At the heart of the campaign was a new commercial for the product featuring a rendition of Team Nekokan’s “Can’t Beat Air Man.” The commercial features an 8-bit sprite of the character known as Rockman in Japan as he fights a possessed McCafe machine, then orders an iced coffee and strolls into a McDonald’s to get it.

Mega Man Dual Override Featured in McDonald’s Japan Campaign

The campaign also has Mega Man mobile wallpapers for customers who want to use their My McDonald’s rewards points. Some of the wallpapers include the logo for the upcoming Mega Man dual override game, and 8-bit versions of Mega Man and Dr. Wily

At the 2025 Game Awards, it was announced that Mega Man would be returning in the aforementioned Dual Override game. That game is due out in 2027. Also, several classic Mega Man games are available now on the Nintendo Switch, PC, XBOX, and Sony PlayStation consoles. The latest release in the franchise is the Mega Man Star Force Legacy Collection. Released in March 2026, the collection includes seven games from the series, additional illustrations, and more.

For more fun McDonald’s crossovers from earlier this year, check out the gaming menu from the Indonesian McDonald’s and the unique AFK tool the branch created.

What did you think of the fun commercial for McDonald’s Japan? Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Classic Rugrats Games Return in Retro Rewind Collection
item.content:encoded:
Rugrats: Retro Rewind Collection features six original titles based on the Nicktoon, released on the original Sony PlayStation, Nintendo Game Boy, Game Boy Color, and Game Boy Advance. The game is now available on Switch, PlayStation, and PC via Steam.

Perhaps the top game in the collection, Rugrats: Search For Reptar, was an original PlayStation game released in 1998. This collection also features the PlayStation releases of Rugrats: Studio Tour and Rugrats in Paris: The Movie. The handheld games released as part of this collection include Rugrats: Time Travelers, The Rugrats Movie, and Rugrats: Castle Capers.

Limited Run Games Launches Rugrats Retro Rewind Collection

As is customary with Limited Run’s vintage collections, there are several bonus features. There is a digital museum where players can check out high-resolution scans of the original manuals and box art. There’s also a music player for the soundtrack, a retro screen filter option, and the ability to rewind and save at any time.

“Whether you’re exploring the Pickles’ backyard or trekking through EuroReptarland, this is the definitive way to experience the games that defined a generation of Nicktoons fans,” the game’s synopsis reads.

This is far from the first Nintendo collection released by Limited Run Games. The company recently released a Ren and Stimpy collection, and a Nickelodeon Splat collection featuring games based on Rocko’s Modern Life, GUTS, and more.

A dip into fun nostalgia for Labor Day weekend in America. So grab your screwdriver, or your stewdriver if you’re Tommy Pickles, and re-experience the world of Rugrats.

Sound off about this story and all others in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: NYT Strands #810 Hints and Answers for Today, May 22, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Strands is a daily word search puzzle game from The New York Times. It is a relatively new game, launched in 2024, in which players seek a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid.

Whether it’s your first time playing or you’re a Strands veteran, today’s tips and strategies can help you find words faster. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Strands, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answers for Strands Game #810 for Friday, May 22, 2026

To play today’s Strands, head to the game’s website.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of Strands. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Strands

Strands is a daily word search that challenges you to find words with a common theme. There’s also a Spanagram that reveals the deeper theme behind the words.

  • Words that are three letters or fewer do not count.
  • Words that reveal the correct theme appear in blue.
  • The Spanagram turns yellow when found.
  • Finding three words that don’t fit the theme will reveal a hint to help you find the proper words.

The goal is to string together as many words as possible. Every letter on the grid gets used! Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Strands can only be played on the New York Times website or on the NYT Games app.

Strands Hints For Today, May 22, 2026

The Strands’ answers to the theme of Game #810 are all terms to describe things that are large in scope.

General Strands Hints For May 22

Without giving today’s Strands answers completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: ____ Games, the makers of Fortnite.
  • Hint 2: The ___ reaches of outer space in Star Trek.
  • Hint 3: A term for beatdown – A molly-___.

Use these clues to narrow down your searches and increase your chances of solving Strands #810 for May 22, 2026.

Words to Help You Find Strand Hints for May 22, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Strands answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you unlock some in-game hints. A few starter words that may help are:

  • LESS
  • PAST
  • PASTE
  • WING
  • WINGS

Today’s Strands Answer For Game #810 On May 22, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answers to today’s Strands below. Check out the answers for past puzzles here.

  • BOUNDLESS
  • ASTRONOMIC
  • WHOPPING
  • IMMENSE
  • VAST
  • EPIC
  • SPANAGRAM: ITSBIG

If you solved today’s Strands without our answers, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

When Is The Next Strands Game After #810?

All New York Times games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours leading up to it, giving players a heads-up. The game on May 23 will be Strands Game #811.

What Is Strands?

Strands is a daily word search puzzle game created by The New York Times. Launched in 2024, Strands challenges players to discover a common theme among words in a 6×8 letter grid. The game’s original concept was developed by Juliette Seive, a research director on The New York Times Games team, with the aim of replicating Wordle’s success. Tracy Bennett has served as the puzzle editor for Strands, overseeing the creation and curation of daily puzzles since the publication’s launch.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Available on iOS & Android
